mnemonics.title: Virus vs Worm
mnemonics.content: VIRUS: Needs Victim In Real User System | WORM: Worm Invades Remote Machines Without user action

mnemonics.title: Trojan Types
mnemonics.content: RATS: Remote Access, Keylogger, Backdoor, Rootkit - All create openings for attackers

mnemonics.title: Malware Prevention
mnemonics.content: SAFER: Security patches, Antivirus, Firewall, Education, Recovery plan

acronyms.usage: Malware that provides remote control over infected systems
Server used to control botnets and direct attacks
First sector of hard drive, target for boot sector viruses
Attack using multiple systems to overwhelm target
Long-term targeted attack often using multiple malware types
Evidence of malware presence like suspicious files, network traffic

visualTips.visualization: Think of a virus as a parasite attaching to a host file like a tapeworm to a human intestine - it cannot survive without the host
Imagine a worm burrowing through soil - it moves independently, leaving copies of itself as it goes, just like network worms spreading through connected computers
Picture the Greek Trojan Horse - looks like a gift (legitimate software) but contains soldiers (malicious code) inside
Imagine a army of zombies (bots) following orders from a central commander (C2 server), all coordinating to attack a single target

analogies.analogy: Virus is like a flu virus spreading through a handshake (human action required), while a worm is like a rumor that spreads automatically through group chats without anyone intentionally sharing it
Just as a trojan horse appeared as a gift to enter Troy, trojan malware appears as a free game or software to enter your computer
A botnet attacking a website is like thousands of people simultaneously calling a single phone number to keep it busy - the line becomes unusable for legitimate callers
A polymorphic virus is like a chameleon changing colors - same dangerous creature, but looks different each time to avoid being recognized
